winner: I drilled a hole through my tomato, but I kept hitting the pit— turns out, it was a cherry-sized drill bit all along.

candidates.text: Why did the tomato refuse to go on the drill? It didn't want to get *sauced* in trouble!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative pun on 'sauced' (related to tomato) and 'drill,' concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: I was told my drill skills were rusty, so I grabbed a tomato to sharpen them—but apparently, even my drill is afraid of ketchup now.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative twist with unexpected connection between drill and tomato; humorous and concise."}

candidates.text: I drilled a hole through my tomato, but I kept hitting the pit— turns out, it was a cherry-sized drill bit all along.

candidates.text: Why did the tomato break up with his drill? He couldn't handle being *drilled* like a *ketchup* factory!

candidates.text: I drilled a hole through this tomato, but honestly, I was just making room for the ketchup.
